mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which compresses corporate profit margins and forces negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the combined Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for leveraged corporations, which suppresses forward equity valuations and generates negative returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of observations where the federal funds rate increases by more than 25 basis points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, which directly lifts equity valuations and produces positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day forward return correlation with quarterly GDP growth revisions remains below 0.10 across all post-1980 data windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, it indicates severe capacity underutilization that triggers deflationary pressures and forces negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Equity returns average positive values during 30% or more of instances where industrial production breaches the 95.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in the unemployment rate and declines in nonfarm payrolls confirm a structural labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand expectations and drives negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, it signals entrenched inflation that erodes real corporate margins and forces negative equity returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns average positive values in more than 45% of observations where the CPI index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year.
